methodology: Tested every SuperBEST v5 operation using its EXACT construction. Grid: xs=[-10,-3,-1,-0.5,-0.1,0.1,0.5,1,3,10], ys same. Positive-domain ops tested on positive subgrid. Tolerance: relative error < 1e-6.

negative_x_detail: (-1.0,2.0): DOMAIN ERROR — expected a positive input, got -1.0
(-1.0,0.5): DOMAIN ERROR — expected a positive input, got -1.0
(-3.0,2.0): DOMAIN ERROR — expected a positive input, got -3.0
mul.correction_note: superbest_v5_table.json says 'all real x,y' for mul cost but domain is x > 0

div.correction_note: Claimed 'x, y > 0' but y < 0 also works

pow.note: Negative exponents n < 0 also fail (ln(n) undefined); integer negative exponents need recip(pow(x,-n))

table_corrections_needed.issue: superbest_v5_table.json says 'all real x,y' for mul cost but domain is x > 0
Claimed 'x, y > 0' but y < 0 also works

conclusion: All SuperBEST v5 constructions compute the correct value within their claimed domain. CRITICAL FINDING: mul(x,y) and div(x,y) require x > 0 because exl(0,x)=ln(x) is only defined for x > 0. superbest.py correctly marks domain as 'x > 0' but the v5_table.json notes and summary text claim 'all real x,y' for mul — this is an inconsistency. The construction itself is correct within x > 0. div(x,y): elsb allows y < 0 (no ln of y), so actual domain is x > 0, y != 0, which is strictly larger than the claimed 'x, y > 0'. add(x,y) = lediv(x, deml(y,1)): deml(y,1)=exp(-y)>0 always, so lediv is always valid. ADD-T1 claim CONFIRMED: works for ALL real x, y. sub(x,y) = lediv(x, eml(y,1)): eml(y,1)=exp(y)>0 always, so lediv is always valid. Works for ALL real x, y. neg(x) = exl(0, deml(x,1)): deml(x,1)=exp(-x)>0 always, so exl is always valid. Works for ALL real x. recip(x) = elsb(0,x): works for x != 0, including negative x. superbest.py says 'x != 0' which is correct. pow(x,n): requires x > 0 AND n > 0 for the given construction eml(exl(ln(n),x),1). Negative exponents need recip(pow(x,-n)).
